topic: Two siblings inherit their grandmother's house
raw_output:
[story 1]

The solicitor’s voice, smooth as polished granite, droned on about probate and stipulations. I gripped the edge of the mahogany table until my knuckles bleached white. "So, you're saying," I managed, my voice dangerously low, "that I have to live in this crumbling mausoleum while you get to pack up the antiques and the good china?" I stared across at my brother, his posture radiating an infuriatingly calm detachment. A muscle twitched in my jaw. "Don't be ridiculous. I'll handle it. I’ll sort out the logistics. Just leave me the deed." I stood up abruptly, the scraping of my chair echoing in the cavernous drawing-room, and didn't wait for a response as I stormed toward the front door.

[story 2]

I paced the length of the dusty foyer, the floorboards protesting under my heavy boots with every aggressive stride. Every shadow in the decaying Victorian house seemed to mock my frustration. "This is an absolute joke," I muttered to myself, kicking a loose piece of plaster across the threshold. The sheer audacity of it—this crumbling relic, thrust upon me like a punishment. I slammed my fist against the doorframe, the wood vibrating with the force of my sudden outburst. "I refuse to be tied to this place!" I spun around, my breath coming in sharp, shallow gasps. My eyes narrowed, fixed on my sibling, whose placid expression only seemed to fuel the rising heat in my chest. "You think you can just take what’s mine?"

[story 3]

The afternoon light, thick with dust motes, illuminated the mess of inherited clutter. I surveyed the room—a suffocating collection of faded velvet and forgotten furniture—and a tight knot formed beneath my ribs. I let out a harsh, short laugh that held no humor whatsoever. "Are you serious?" I demanded, my voice tight with barely contained fury. I snatched a heavy, tarnished silver picture frame off a mantelpiece, the metal cold against my palm, and hurled it toward the fireplace. It struck the hearth with a sharp clang, sending a shower of soot into the air. I watched the debris settle, breathing heavily, my entire body vibrating with suppressed agitation. "This isn't a gift; it’s an imposition. I won't tolerate this arrangement."
